title: Quantum Speedup Learning: A Formal Framework for Enhanced Problem Solving
abstract: Quantum computing promises to revolutionize computational efficiency by leveraging quantum mechanics principles. Building on the formal framework for speedup learning from problems and solutions, this paper explores the integration of quantum computing theory to enhance problem-solving efficiency. We propose a quantum-enhanced learning algorithm that leverages quantum parallelism and superposition to accelerate the acquisition of control rules and macro-operators. Our methodology involves quantum simulations of symbolic integration and the Eight Puzzle domain, demonstrating significant speedup compared to classical approaches. Theoretical proofs and empirical results indicate that quantum speedup learning can substantially reduce computational complexity, offering a new paradigm for machine learning in quantum computing environments. This work bridges the gap between quantum computing and machine learning, paving the way for future research in quantum-enhanced artificial intelligence.
